item.title: I like it, but...
item.content:encoded:
So to be clear, the barrier is impenetrable, but STF Beta-8 is expected to regularly get people in or out of it? -1

This isn't even a difficult mistake to correct. Just say that it seemed to be impenetrable upon discovery, but someone at the Foundation found a way through. I want to like this scip, but such an obvious inconsistency right at the start is a big turn off. (Also the tone is a little awkward, and I predicted the twist ending as soon as the transmission logs specified a feminine voice, but these are less significant issues.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hahaha, excellent! That ending got a genuine surprised laugh out of me, and I do like the rest of the article as well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The containment procedures make no sense at all. How is STF Beta-8 supposed to expel unauthorized people entering SCP-3490, if it's protected by an impenetrable wall?

I'll upvote this in a heartbeat if this is adressed in any way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i actually, really liked this. at first it was going to be a novote as the "cutesy" SCP's usually dont interest me but the end of the log? yeah that's gonna be a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image Credits:

SCP-3490 - https://www.flickr.com/photos/horiavarlan/4272009397

Let's hope this doesn't fall into the pits of hell again D:
